zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Leighton是John Leighton和Mrs. John Leighton的女兒，於大約1480年出生在英國史洛普爾華特萊斯波洛。她與William Mason II結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1540年之後。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Leighton是John Leighton和Mrs. John Leighton的女儿，于大约1480年出生在英国史洛普爾華特萊斯波洛。她与William Mason II结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1540年之后。

nameMeanings.name: Leighton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Leighton or Layton. Most, as for example those in Bedfordshire, Cambridgeshire, Cheshire, Lancashire, and Shropshire, are named with Old English lēac ‘leek’ + tūn ‘settlement’, but Layton in Durham derives from Old English ge-lād ‘road’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The English pronunciation of Leighton is usually /lɛ:tən/ or /leitən/ (also spelled Layton and Leaton ), but it can also be heard as /laitən/ or as /li:tən/ (also spelled Leaton ), and occasionally as /lɛtən/ (spelled Letton ).

variants.name: Slayton
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of Leighton (compare Layton ) with the addition of initial S-. Alternatively, perhaps a variant of Sladen or of Sleightholme, principally a habitational name from Sleightholme Dale in Kirkbymoorside, but perhaps occasionally from Sleightholme in Bowes or the lost Sleightholme in Lythe. The placenames derive from Old Norse sléttr ‘smooth, level’ + holmr ‘small island, water meadow’. This surname is now very rare in Britain. Compare Slaydon .

variants.name: Laughton
meanings: habitational name from Laughton en le Morthen in Yorkshire, Laughton in Leicestershire or any of the two places called Laughton in Lincolnshire (one near Gainsborough and another near Folkingham). The places are named with Old English lēac ‘leek’ + tūn ‘enclosure’, meaning ‘village or farm where leeks are grown’. It may sometimes have alternated with Leighton , which is mostly from placenames with the same etymology.

variants.name: Letton
meanings: English: habitational name, most probably from Letton in Norfolk or Latton in Essex. It could also be a variant of Leighton or Laughton . Most of the places so named have the same etymology as Letton and Latton, i.e. ‘farm where leeks are grown’ (Old English lēac-tūn). Another possibility is that it is a variant of Latten .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Leighton was born about 1480, in Wattlesborough, Shropshire, England as the daughter of John Leighton and Mrs. John Leighton. She married William Mason II. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. She died after 1540.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Leighton est née environ 1480, à Wattlesborough, Shropshire, Angleterre, fille de John Leighton et Mrs. John Leighton. Elle a épousé William Mason II. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée après 1540.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Leighton wurde um 1480 in Wattlesborough, Shropshire, England, als Tochter von John Leighton und Mrs. John Leighton geboren. Sie hat William Mason II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb nach 1540.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Leighton è nata il circa 1480 nel Wattlesborough, Shropshire, England come figlia di John Leighton e Mrs. John Leighton. Lei sposato William Mason II. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 5 figlie. Morì dopo il 1540.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Leightonは約1480年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアワットレスボロウでJohn LeightonとMrs. John Leightonの娘として生まれた。William Mason IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1540年以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Leighton(은)는 대략 1480년 영국 슈럽실르 왓틀레스버루그h에서 John Leighton(와)과 Mrs. John Leighton의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 William Mason II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1540년 이후 사망했다.
